marketState: neutral, cautiously bullish medium-term (hot weather + crowded short COT vs. storage surplus and a bearish daily trend)
summary: Front price ($2.911) sits almost exactly at model fair value ($2.923, spread -$0.012, FAIR status), so there is no near-term mispricing edge today. The strongest signal is weather: demand is +13% above 5-year normal over 1-7 days and +21.8% over 8-16 days, with the medium window near the top of its seasonal range (rangePos 0.97), and the fair-value curve itself drifts up from $2.923 now to $3.042 by +21 days, implying the model expects the heat-driven demand pull to firm prices. That bullish read is offset by a +180 Bcf storage surplus vs the 5-year average, a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week, and seasonality showing only a 38% chance of higher prices at the 3-4 week mark with low reliability (59pp 10-90% spread). Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a stretched positioning that could fuel a short-covering rally. Technicals split by timeframe - 15m/1h lean neutral, while 4h/1d/1w show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross and negative daily/weekly MACD; futuresCurve data was unavailable (null) to confirm term-structure positioning.
